textDisplay: I really liked this movie! Sometimes the trailer is better than the movie or it gives away too much. That really isn’t the case here, movie lives up to the energy of this trailer! The movie injects humor that is familiar to the franchise but this was a much darker, grittier film than before. Definitely worth seeing on the big screen!

Okay so I think we need to get out of the box for scream 7, here’s what I’m thinking: 

What if the opening scene to scream 7 is Sidney and Mark’s house. We see Sidney and Mark take off and describing every detail to the babysitter beforehand. 

Later that night….They drive home from a night out just like Cassie becker’s parents in the original scream (1996). But this time the opening kill is the babysitter who gets the call. In that way they could tie in some “when a stranger calls” elements. So we never see Sidney’s children because they are sleeping. Meanwhile it all takes place just like in scream 1996/when a stranger calls. Kind a like a full circle moment.

Plus it’s different because in the original “when a stranger calls. The children dies, but this time it’s the babysitter.

Later in the movie we see Mark and Sidney team up to make a private police investigation, and so they make a whole killer board. They invite the survivors from scream 6 to help (and all the other survivors from past movies). They track down clues along the way and find themselves questioning people like Lesley macher (stu’s sister) and learn that he is in fact alive but is in fact not the killer - or is he?…..
The whole movie is about tracking the killer down while people are dropping dead left to right until the 3rd act. 

The killer is revealed and it is…….someone we have never learned about until now, but has had a finger in every ghostface attack since the very beginning. We could se flashbacks from every single 3rd act that took place.

The same very someone that has been obsessed with Sidney Prescott since she was a little girl. HER NEXT DOOR NEIGHBOR. The child molester, that lived on elm.  Sounds familiar? (No supernatural stuff tho)

Everyone thinks that Samara Weaving is the opening kill but she is in fact the main killer. She is the baby sister of Drew Barry more who went into the Foster system after the murder of her older sister and her mum having a complete psychotic breakdown and father leaving her and remarriage. Sidney is in the last scene of the movie when Samara kills Gale and phones Sidney from her phone. Sidney runs upstairs to check on her children who are missing from their beds and Kincaid is seen lying on the kitchen floor with his throat slashed bleeding out and the back door swinging. This is when we realise there is a second killer before Sam takes out Samara. Everyone else in the movie dies including Kirby and Tara its a complete bloodbath. The finale doesn't actually take place in the shrine/abandoned movie theatre but in an old factory newspaper facility on the other side of the same building which has been hidden for years.

Stuff this new Ghostface is most likely to do (theories):

Considering that this Ghostface is actually going to be serious and perhaps the most deadliest ever, there are some things this Ghostface is more likely to do, or at least some twists in Scream 6.

1. Ghostface might not reveal his true identity. Whoever is being hunted by Ghostface, it's felt as if you're being chased by a literal "ghost". As this Ghostface may be the most dangerous since he plans to not repeat the same mistakes with the previous ones, he may not try to reveal his identity. The one problem with most Ghostface is that although the reveal is very climatic, it looses the suspense and fear as our heroes come to realize that this Ghostface is just human. By not revealing who he/she is, it heightens the fear of the victims and makes them more exposed to making irrational decisions, as if they're going against something more "supernatural".  Most would not agree with this choice as the Scream films are known as iconic horror whodunnits, however they are also known for subverting expectations, so if our expectations are subverted by not knowing who Ghostface is by the end, then that might be refreshing.

2. The killer may not be related to the main characters at all. As most of you have known, the prime suspect of the killer is always connected to the main characters, or to put it more simply who's in the poster. Perhaps the biggest twist of in this Scream film is that the killer's reveal may not be a character who's interacted with the main characters at all during the movie. For all I know, this character could be a background character watching and successfully stalk his prey, plotting his next move. This would be a wonderful choice as the main characters would expect that the killer may be a supposed ally of theirs but in reality is just some guy they've passed by, someone that the main characters wouldn't have known in their lifetimes but could be the opposite for the killer. It would be a great subversion of expectations and a good choice for a Ghostface who's brought up to be the most deadliest.
 
Those are all the theories I have about Scream 6. Let me know about your theories below and reply if you think I could be correct or not.

Her shooting at the door was so reminiscent of shooting through the mirrors in Scream 3. Also maybe it's just my imagination but it seemed like a cop move (for all the Kirby theorists ❤) when Ghostface moved Gale's arm out of the way so she couldn't shoot him. (But seriously Gale, don't stand so close to the door!)  Also, on the subway, it appears there are 3 Ghostfaces?? 2 on the left in the same shot, and 1 on the right in the next shot. It's totally possible one moved to the right and it wasnt portraying 3 simultaneous Ghostface. For all we know those are just more costumes anyway since everyone else on the subway is wearing a mask, doesn't necessarily mean they are Ghostface. Personally I'm kind of hoping for a Ghostface cult and that's what I'm picking up on in this trailer. I just don't want a repeat of Scream 5 where there is supposed to be something mindblowingly different about this killer and it turns out they're just another Ghostface. Everyone thinks that Samara Weaving is the opening kill but she is in fact the main killer. She is the baby sister of Drew Barry more who went into the Foster system after the murder of her older sister and her mum having a complete psychotic breakdown and father leaving her and remarriage. I wasn't going to see this one because I'm a little tired of the nostalgia bait, but it actually looks interesting.

It was a strong choice to open it with a public shooting, both because it mirrors and draws upon modern fears (modern teenagers know they don't have to be home alone at night to suffer a murder by a lone maniac anymore, sadly) as well as subverts slasher tropes which generally rely on the conceit that part of the victims' danger is that they're far from other people who can help. Showing that even in a brightly lit, populated area they are still in danger heightens the menace.

I laughed when she just immediately answers the phone with, "You know I've been through this like ten times, right?" with zero hesitation lol.

I am worried about the whole "cult" aspect. One of the go-to-cliches for any horror sequel is to create a cult around the villain and it usually doesn't end well, winding up being more corny and desperate than scary. But I guess they could do some interesting commentary with it if they play their cards right, especially if they draw parallels between Ghostface and mass shooters.

This looks really bad. The series is not "limping on" it's wearing the dismembered body parts of the original, trying to disguise itself as a competent, justifiable entry to the series. You know when someone says to you "oh I only watch GOOD movies" in a really smug tone, or something along those lines, that's literally condensed into the one word "elevated" when in the previous film J.O's character says "elevated horror", the fact that the writers unironically used that in their film is seriously telling of how smug and stupid the writing is and don't tell me it was intentionally smug because that whole moment was meant as a "twist" where the helpless girl being called is actually super knowledgeable and intelligent in regrades to horror films outshining the horror icon who's calling. It was meant to endear the audience to her character. So many moments of that film had bits like this where one can easily break down and explain the poor writing. This film has nothing new, nothing special to see, take away the dramatic music in the trailer and what are you left with? The most impressive stunt/intense scene in the trailer to show (and you know that trailer creators these days want to flex EVERYTHING in the movie to maximise ticket sales at any cost) was a scene ripped straight out of that forgettable movie "nerve" where a character crosses between two buildings at a dangerous height, its also kind of used in "truth or dare". Basically just not interesting at all to see at this point. Also Gail doing the already overused " blah blah you're like the x number of people to do this" only for this killer to say THE MOST BASIC RESPONSE EVER. Hilarious.
If you enjoy these movies shame on you, this film only exists because of nostalgia and pandering not creativity and passion. But hey, get your "Jenna Ortega in a horror movie" fix I sure don't know what else you get out of these movies.

My SCREAM VI theories:

1- Mrs. who caused Randy's death. Loomis being Billy's mother. Hearing this, Randy's nephew Chad may be one of the Ghostfaces as a result of his feeling of revenge against Sam and Tara, who are the blood of Loomis, I don't think Mindy will be, and Chad was too stagnant in the trailers. (Low but possible)

  2- Sam, who saw his father Billy Loomis' hallucinations and lost his sanity, appeared in Ghostface in the third act!
  cf. His moves while killing Richie in the previous movie show that he can do the job. His motivation is maybe Billy Loomis' mentally deranged daughter!?

3- Kirby Reed's "different" return!
As a result of the interpretation of the "something different" slogan that is repeated throughout the trailer, I think it is a fire of unfinished revenge in the past, not for the fans and fame. Kirby fits this concept as well, and another person who fits is Kirby's co-star Charlie from Scream 4.

4- Who uses the pump so well in that market? Just in case, Detective Kincaid.

5- How many Ghostfaces can there be?
There were 2 killers in 4 of the 5 movie series. However, I say that there will be only one killer in this movie, or there will be 3 or more than 3 killers, because this time it is "different."

6- Is Stu Macher returning?
I think the claims that Stu died in the first movie are false, he will return in the seventh movie at the latest. Films 5-6-7 will be a trilogy. Movie 5 released last year will debut, movie number 6 of this trailer will be sequal, and the movie that will be released in 2024 or later will be Trilogy.

7- Will Gale live?
I'm sorry I'm waiting for Gale's farewell. In Sidney's absence, Gale has to take charge and his death is likely in the last 30 minutes of the movie.

8- Why is Sydney absent?
According to the explanations, there was no agreement on the fee bla bla. I think Sidney is not in this movie for the series and by the end of the movie they will definitely give the green light that he will be in the 7th movie. Fast & Furious 9's final scene, for example, is the green light for Brian O'Conner's return in the new movie.

All these theories are mine. If the second trailer comes out in February, I will arrange it according to the progress. ✍️

Not to offend anyone who is interested in the new rip-off horror movies but, I really think that Hollywood have lost their way. They should take a break for even a moment and look outside of their big budget movies with all of the stars in them. I get it, they only want money, but Art was never about money, it was about the expression of ones self through a visual medium. Now, take it from someone who is in the middle of their first feature and appreciates the work David Lynch and Stanley Kubrick, I am from Australia, and I wouldn't be surprised if the Hollywood big wigs have never seen an Australian movie, or any non-American production before. But if there is anyone reading this who reading this who is even part of any operation to do with movies then please would you take some time to look at some movies that maybe you wouldn't normally watch. Try:

Eraserhead
The Cook, The Thief, His Wife, And Her Lover
Bad Boy Bubby
These Final Hours
Monsters
Acute Misfortune
The Stranger
The Rover.

These are just some of the many Low-Budget movies that are full of real, raw, good Art. I'm not saying that you're and asshole if you enjoy Marvel movies, or that you have bad taste if you look forward to the work of James Gunn, but I just think that Hollywood and any other big production company, just need to stop and think that maybe there is something else in store for the future of cinema.

And if you are just someone who is reading this and finds any of this interesting, check some of these films out, they might not be something that you enjoy, or they could be the genre you've been looking for. I hope I have made a valuable point and with that, take care everybody...

Hmm, I think one of the main villains is Dermot Mulroney's police character.  Keep in mind he's pretty much the only character that hasn't been named yet, has no lines in the trailer, just two quick shots in the trailer.  One of which shows his character with a pistol in his right hand and cell phone to his left ear; along with a cut or abrasion on his forehead.  This could indicate he's on a cat/mouse call with the killer, is stalking one of the main characters and is being taunted by them, or calling for backup.  All that being said, notice I pointed out the pistol being in his right hand.  The shots of this Ghostface show him to be right handed.  Also, some further possible evidence to back up my theory is the beginning sequence of the trailer.  The killer is about Dermot's height, handles the shotgun in a very similar fashion that Dermot did in the western Young Guns (his character in that preferred a shotgun and was right handed), and the way Ghostface maneuvers and ducks around the store suggests police training.  I think the character could possibly be an older brother or cousin to Stu (the actors are only seven years apart) who at one point may've been a California state trooper or county police officer during the events of Scream and has been slowly, but obsessively following every incident.  At some point he learned about Roman, even could've had a brief relationship with Billy's mother.  A scenario like that would have more than likely warped his world view and for whatever reason has decided to be part of this new demented spree, going after survivors.  Though he could just simply be Kirby's partner...but this theory could also be a major nod to the days when Dewey was thought to be a villain lol

The subway scene is not long into the movie, it results in Sam and Tara getting separated from the twins, leading to the convenience store scene after which Kirby is introduced to the movie (either rescuing the girls or arriving after a typical Ghostface disappearance to investigate the aftermath). Also prediction, but I'm less certain of this: Kirby and/or Tara will be the killer; Tara, killer or not, will die toward the end.

Funny story,  I actually met Brandon Call at a shuffleboard tournament years back.
I was star-struck. 
I got my first taste of the Hollywood limelight, and I was in a state of transcendental ecstatic bliss. My chest swayed, my eyes rolled back in my head and I gasped, "Brandon Call, right? From Step by Step."
He acknowledged me with a gleeful smirk and a proud nod.
"That's me."
Sprawled on the concrete, writhing in ecstacy, I managed to garble out a few more words, "they don't make shows like that anymore."
He agreed and proceeded down the boardwalk hand in hand with his supple wife. 
It's impressively gracious that a man of such caliber would ever grace my presence, let alone stoop to the level of the common man for a humble chat.

Since then, I've reflected back on that day with great reverence pondering where would we be now if back when even before they made shows like that, we all got to experience a certain level of precision entertainment that people were never able to get when they can't even watch it before they did it now!

I'll miss those days. 🫖 ❤️ 🍿

*KILLER IDENTITY THEORY*
To figure out the killer, you have to ask the following questions (aside from what we find out in this movie):
- Why are they so angry at the group (especially Sam) they want to kill them, following them all the way to New York?  Why not go after Sidney?
- How did they get so much money to make a fancy Ghostface collection room?
- How did they get their hands on each costume from the original killers?
- Why did Kirby suddenly return?
- Who is the artist?
- Could it be a group of people?  A Ghostface cult?  An organization of those with different talents and resources?
- Are they related to someone who died by Billy's hands? Sins of the father theme?

This feels like revenge more than the recycled "I did it for fame" motive that was done the past two movies.  It feels personal this time.  Look at the obsessive amount of details, the hard effort this killer went through to study the killers' past mistakes. It's on a whole new level beyond any of the past killers, even the personal agendas involved with Sydney somehow. I think it's someone who either was a victim, or lost someone killed by a past Ghostface killer, who wants to end it all so no one else dies because the group is full of walking targets.

I have never watched a Scream movie. I don't watch many horror movies in general. I am not the target audience, and am in no way entitled to having a Scream movie that is catered to me.

WITH THAT BEING SAID this trailer bugs me. I'd be genuinely interested in watching a movie about a slasher, especially one as iconic as Ghost Face, but the logic in these scenes is maddening.

I don't care if he does or doesn't have super powers, the only reason he is getting any kills is either because the characters are idiotic, or because the cinematography covers up his movements to make it look like his movements make sense. Yes, people act stupid when they are scared IRL, but in a horror movie the scenes should make me anxious or scared, not frustrated. As such its better to make the characters unnaturally smart and make the killer smarter than to make the characters dumb and the killer average.

His name is Ghost Face, he should be stalking and ambushing people, not John Wicking people's guns out of their hands in public.

I sincerely hope the original Scream fans have a good time though.

Stop giving away the movie in ads! Start the ad showing the real movie (not the adhd commercial jump cut) from the girls just outside the store in a panic running to it to try and get help, up until the guy confronting Ghostface asks "is there a problem?" Fade fully to black and show the release date in silence and let the audience enjoy the tension build up, imagining what happens, and let them enjoy what they pay for at the theater or online without the film being spoiled and showing major scenes. The fun of movies, especially horror, is not knowing what's going to happen, you don't have to give a pay off in the trailer, that's ment for us when we watch it.

1:12- When the curtain pulled up to reveal the shrine, I thought for a split-second "What if there were hypothetically a lot more killers working together, like a Ghostface cult?!" This would build on the "radical fandom" theme in the last movie. 

It would be a perfectly believable evolution from how the Ghostface killer's iconography spread over time thanks to the Stab series. Think of it like a commentary on how the line becomes blurred between fandom and religious fanaticism.

Ex- The Slenderman stabbings. I'd be impressed if it turned out that EVERY new character was actually in on the cult except for Jenna Ortega's. She wouldn't learn this until after all her friends appear to have been killed off by the main Ghostface.

Like, imagine if their deaths were all staged (though the other victims & bystanders they killed along the way were real) and they reveal to Jenna at the end "Surprise! This has been our production for a Stab fan-film that will immortalize us forever!

And YOUR death is the finale! EVERYONE will follow our example!" Jenna would survive, of course, but Sidney and/or Gale would likely sacrifice themselves to save her, thereby killing off another original lead to further prop up the new blood.

The final twist, however, is that Jenna learns the cult prepared for being defeated. They live-streamed the entire event along with the existing footage. Their fan-film is complete and has now been automatically posted online for all to see.

Dozens-hundreds of Stab fans are inspired to join the cult's cause, donning masks and beginning their own killing sprees which they similarly record to post. Thus, one last sequel is set up wherein Ghostface's reign of terror officially goes global.
The way this ghost face claims to be different, we need one that has a supernatural ability. I think that's the way to carry this franchise to a whole new level.

I have to say... I'm thoroughly impressed. For the record, as a Scream fan I'm not really a hardcore one. I love the first, like the second until the end, don't care for the third, thought the fourth was okay, loved the first two seasons of the TV series and hated the third and especially did not like the fifth so I wasn't really looking to watch this one but... this trailer has me kind of sold. I love the darker vibe it aims for, how the change in setting seems to really lend itself to creative creepy setpieces and honestly while I don't want to bring up controversy? In a weird way I feel given the circumstances it basically pushed the crew to really try something new which is something I've been wanting from this series for a good while now. Overall, I'm actually pretty intrigued.

This is _so_ disappointing. It  just seems like a repackage of a script that was already floating around. Part of the charm of the Scream franchise is its black comedy/camp slasher atmosphere, and it's that specific combination that made it _so_ successful among its peers. This is just as bad as _Scary Movie,_ except being an entirely comedic cut-and-paste, this is just an entirely horror cut-and-paste -- this trailer wouldn't need much tweaking at all to be a replacement for one of the most recent Saw franchise films. It doesn't look like a _bad_ movie; it just doesn't look like a _Scream_ movie.

The only way this could be redeemed would be for this super-serious trailer to not actually be indicative of the movie. If there's one overarching theme, it's always been that Scream movies play on and subvert the popular horror movie tropes without becoming a self-serious horror flick or a dumbed-down parody. So if this is a send up of gritty, realistic horror, it could fit, but not as the serious film it presents itself as in the trailer.

This trend of the damaged mask trope with the killers in legacy horror movie sequels is honestly not getting old at all, it's just so aesthetically pleasing and really lends to the nature of "this time around I'm too beat up and now tired of your shit".

For those who dont know, it started with I think Chucky(more of a face than mask but same premise) than continued with things like Halloween.

Edit: Unfortunately have to give additional context since the usual YT comment section shenanigans are starting to happen. When I say Chucky I meant the franchise not the film, even though I don't know how this could be misinterpreted given that the first Chucky isn't called Chucky but Child's Play. And when I say Halloween I also meant the franchise, but given the fact the there's like 3 or 4 films simply titled Halloween, I get the confusion. Anyways the trope, I first saw it in Bride Of Chucky, than I saw it in Halloween Kills, and now we see it in Scream 6. I don't count Jason in FT13th because his mask was altered for every film without much narrative tie-in for many of the films. FT13th part 9 is the closest to being apart of the trope but hard to count considering he gets *spoiler alert below*

Camera guy from scream 1, detectives from scream 2, Christine in scream 3, Marnie and the cops in scream 4 and the hospital security guard in scream 5.
